cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the movies. Let me start by going through the information given step by step. First, Leslie woke up at 6am. Then, Susan saw Leslie on the rooftop from 6am to 7am. So, between 6 and 7 am, Leslie was definitely on the rooftop. That means Leslie wasn't free during that time to go to the movies. So option D, which is 6am to 7am, can be ruled out because Leslie was already accounted for during that period. Next, Mary saw Leslie working at the office from 12pm to 2pm. So between noon and 2pm, Leslie was at the office. Therefore, Leslie couldn't have gone to the movies during that time either. Option C is 12pm to 2pm, which is when Leslie was working, so that's not possible. Then, Steven saw Leslie at the cafe getting coffee from 2pm to 7pm. That's a long stretch of time. So from 2pm until 7pm, Leslie was at the cafe. The movies closed after 7pm, so the latest Leslie could have gone to the movies is 7pm. But since Leslie was at the cafe from 2pm to 7pm, they couldn't have gone to the movies during that time either. So option B, 2pm to 7pm, is also out because Leslie was at the cafe then. So the only time left is between 7am and 12pm. Let's check if that's feasible. Leslie was on the rooftop until 7am. After that, there's a window from 7am until 12pm when there's no mention of Leslie's activities. Mary saw Leslie at the office starting at 12pm, so before that, Leslie was free. Therefore, Leslie could have gone to the movies anytime between 7am and 12pm. That matches option A. Wait, but let me double-check. The movies closed after 7pm, so theoretically, the movie could have started as late as 7pm.
